license_note: Line texts authored in-repo for this project (original prose). Engines: Dia-1.6B-0626 Apache-2.0, Qwen3-TTS-VoiceDesign Apache-2.0, MOSS-TTS-8.5B Apache-2.0, LongCat-AudioDiT-3.5B MIT.

lines.owner_audit: 2026-07-17: emotion obvious; not grief — fear and concern over impending loss (plays as the scene before grief_herenow_02). Relabeled.
lines.text: No, no, stay with me. Stay with me, please. Don't you close your eyes. Not now.

direction.design: A middle-aged man's voice, rough with panic and love, in American English.
direction.instruct: Speak with raw, immediate grief and desperation, voice cracking, pleading through tears.

lines.text: She was just here. She was just... I was holding her hand a minute ago.

direction.design: A middle-aged man's voice, hollow and stunned, in American English.
direction.instruct: Speak in stunned, broken disbelief, trailing off, on the edge of tears.

lines.text: Walk away from this. Right now. You do not want to find out what happens if you stay.

direction.design: A calm adult man's voice, low and controlled, in American English.
direction.instruct: Speak as a stern, level warning — quiet, deliberate, leaving no doubt.

lines.owner_audit: 2026-07-17: conveyance fantastic (youthful jubilance), but the fairy voicing is niche-extreme — redesign the voice description next campaign, keep the register.
lines.text: We did it, we did it! Oh, stars and sparkles, we actually did it!

direction.design: A bright, airy young woman's voice, playful and light, in American English.
direction.instruct: Speak with bubbly, giddy, musical delight, laughing through the words.

lines.text: Then Kevin puffed out his chest and said, "Nobody skates past me. Nobody." And the whole locker room believed him.

direction.design: A warm adult female audiobook narrator, in American English.
direction.instruct: Narrate warmly; when quoting the teenage boy Kevin, lower and roughen your voice to play him, then return to your own voice for the last sentence.

lines.text: They took her from me. Every one of them walked away smiling. I remember every face.

direction.instruct: A deep-voiced grown man speaking slowly through cold, contained grief that has hardened into resolve — quiet, dark, steady, in American English.

lines.text: Sit down. Finish your drink. We're going to talk about what you did, nice and slow.

direction.instruct: A gravelly older man's voice, stone cold and unhurried, like a black-and-white noir gangster — menacing without ever raising his voice, in American English.

lines.owner_audit: 2026-07-17: very good — 'the longshot job called' energy, not cup-final energy; victory too strong a word. Relabeled excited_good_news.
lines.text: Ha! Did you see that? Thirty years I've waited for this. Somebody pinch me.

direction.instruct: An ordinary middle-aged man in a bar the moment his team finally wins — delighted, laughing, a little breathless, completely genuine, in American English.

lines.text: The train left the valley just after noon, and the fields unrolled beside it like a long green letter that nobody needed to read.

direction.instruct: A warm, natural middle-aged male audiobook narrator, calm and engaged, even pacing, in American English.

lines.text: His grandmother waved him closer and whispered, "The garden remembers everyone who ever tended it." Then she smiled and went back to her tea.

direction.instruct: A male audiobook narrator with a warm even voice; when quoting the grandmother, soften and age your voice into a gentle whisper to play her, then return to your own narrator voice, in American English.

lines.text: We won! We won, we won, WE WON! The championship is ours!

direction.render_text: [S1] We won! (laughs) We won, we won, WE WON! The championship is ours!

lines.text: Give it back. Now. I will not ask twice.

direction.render_text: [S1] Give it back. Now. I will not. Ask. Twice.

lines.text: It's been six months. I finally boxed up her coat yesterday. I kept one glove.

direction.render_text: [S1] It's been six months. I finally boxed up her coat yesterday. (sighs) I kept one glove.

lines.text: Shh. Stay behind me. Keep your head down and hold my hand. We're going to be fine.

direction.render_text: [S1] Shh. Stay behind me. Keep your head down and hold my hand. We're going to be fine.

lines.text: By morning the tide had gone out so far that the boats lay on their sides in the sand, patient as sleeping animals.

direction.render_text: [S1] By morning the tide had gone out so far that the boats lay on their sides in the sand, patient as sleeping animals.
